hero.name: Kurt Dereck Kusch Sepúlveda
hero.description: I develop web solutions focused on backend, modular architecture, and cloud deployment, integrating modern interfaces when the product requires it.

about.title: About
about.paragraph1: Computer Engineering & Informatics professional with experience in developing web systems focused on administrative management, sales platforms, and booking systems.
about.paragraph2: With a Junior Backend / Full Stack focus, I specialize in backend development with Python and Django, Django REST Framework, and PHP with Laravel, working with relational databases such as PostgreSQL and MySQL.
about.paragraph3: I also have experience in modern frontend using React and TypeScript, and in deploying applications on cloud platforms like Render and Vercel. My focus is to build maintainable, scalable, and well-structured software, following engineering best practices.

cleanCode.title: Clean Code
cleanCode.description: Readable and maintainable code with clear names, single responsibility, and consistent conventions.

modularArchitecture.title: Modular Architecture
modularArchitecture.description: Decoupled and reusable components to evolve the system without unnecessary technical debt.

realOptimization.title: Real Optimization
realOptimization.description: I measure and optimize render, bundle size, and perceived load to ensure smooth UX on any device.

continuousLearning.title: Continuous Learning
continuousLearning.description: I adapt quickly to new tools and approaches to solve complex problems with technical criteria.

projects.title: Featured
projects.description: A selection of projects that demonstrate my technical skills in software development, system architecture, and complex problem solving.

git-simulator.title: Git Simulator in C
git-simulator.shortDescription: Implementation of init, add, commit, log, and checkout commands using data structures and file handling.
git-simulator.fullDescription:
Complete system simulating Git's internal workings, implementing fundamental version control system commands.

This project demonstrates deep understanding of:
- Complex data structures
- File handling in C
- Hash algorithms and state management
- Version control system design

The simulator allows creating repositories, tracking changes, making commits, and navigating between different code versions, replicating Git's core behavior.
highlights: Complete implementation of init, add, commit, log, and checkout
Hash system for commit identification
Efficient memory management
Binary and text file handling

dataFlow: User executes a command in the CLI.
Parser interprets arguments and validates rules.
Engine updates index and commit objects on disk.
Repository state is reflected in log and checkout.

order-taking-app.title: Order-Taking Application
order-taking-app.shortDescription: System for field sales representatives to register orders for a food distribution company.
order-taking-app.fullDescription:
Mobile/web application designed to optimize the field order-taking process for food distributors.

Main features:
- Intuitive interface for field sales representatives
- Product catalog with quick search
- Customer and route management
- Offline/online data synchronization
- Report and statistics generation

The application significantly improves sales efficiency, reduces order errors, and facilitates centralized sales management.
highlights: Responsive interface optimized for tablets
Offline mode with automatic synchronization
Real-time inventory validation
Notification system for updates

dataFlow: Sales representative registers orders from mobile interface.
App validates stock and customer locally for immediate response.
Synchronization service sends changes to REST API.
SQL database persists orders and publishes status for reporting.

portfolio-website.title: Personal Portfolio
portfolio-website.shortDescription: Professional SPA to present profile, tech stack, and projects with modular architecture, technical SEO, and performance optimization.
portfolio-website.fullDescription:
SPA web system designed to communicate software engineering experience through a modern and maintainable interface.

Problem it solves:
- Centralize experience, stack, and technical evidence in one platform.
- Present projects with architectural context and operational flow.
- Improve professional visibility.

Technical approach:
- Modular layered architecture (pages, sections, components, data, constants, config).
- Optimized rendering with lazy loading, code splitting, and component memoization.
- Interactive experience with controlled animations and reduced motion support.
- Contact integration with EmailJS and anti-spam validations on frontend.
highlights: Scalable modular architecture with clear separation of concerns
Manual bundle splitting in Vite to improve caching and initial load time
ErrorBoundary, lazy loading, and fallback UI for production resilience
Contact form with typed validations, honeypot, and rate limiting
Applied accessibility (skip link, associated labels, key ARIA attributes)
Complete technical SEO: Open Graph, Twitter Cards, canonical, and JSON-LD
Strict TypeScript usage to reduce errors and improve maintainability

dataFlow: User enters the SPA and router loads Home with composable sections.
In Projects, system applies typed filters and renders optimized cards.
When opening a project, it's queried by id in data layer to build technical view.
In Contact, fields and anti-spam are validated; then payload is sent to EmailJS.
Usage metrics are reported with Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights.

dap-transport-routing-system.title: DAP Transport and Shift Routing Management System
dap-transport-routing-system.shortDescription: Internal Laravel web application for managing transport shifts, optimizing routes, and generating operational reports with map visualization.
dap-transport-routing-system.fullDescription:
Internal web application developed with Laravel to manage employees, pickup addresses, common locations, and airport transport shifts.

Problem it solves:
- Complex coordination of staff transport with multiple pickup points and arrival time constraints.
- Reduction of manual planning errors and increased operational traceability.
- Standardization of operational output for field execution.

Technical approach:
- Structured assignment of employees and addresses per shift.
- Pickup order optimization with real road network distances.
- Automatic calculation of stop times to meet target arrival time.
- Planning export in Excel, PDF, and PNG, with geographic map visualization support.
highlights: Custom optimization engine (TieredNearestNeighborOptimizer) with 2-opt and or-opt refinement
Distance matrix with OSRM and automatic Haversine fallback on API failures
Geometry persistence per segment in ruta_puntos.geometry for immediate map rendering
Automatic schedule recalculation when optimizing or manually reordering points
Flexible shift model with fixed/dynamic intervals based on distance
Common location handling without losing route temporal coherence
Complete operational pipeline: registration/import, optimization, visualization, and export
Decoupled MVC architecture prepared for functional evolution

architecture: Presentation: Blade + UI Components + Leaflet Map
Data: Relational model (employees, shifts, routes, route_points)
dataFlow: User registers or imports georeferenced employees and addresses.
Defines shift with date, target arrival time, and interval parameters.
Selects participating staff and executes route optimization.
System queries OSRM, calculates pickup order, and persists route + route_points.
Estimated times per point are calculated based on target arrival.
User visualizes map and spreadsheet, manually adjusts order if needed.
When saving adjustments, times are recalculated and exported in Excel, PDF, or PNG.

lash-kingdom-booking-platform.title: Lash Kingdom - Beauty Salon Booking Platform
lash-kingdom-booking-platform.shortDescription: Django web platform for online bookings, operational management, and availability control with double-booking protection.
lash-kingdom-booking-platform.fullDescription:
Lash Kingdom is a Django-based appointment management web platform for a salon specialized in eyelash extensions and beauty treatments.

Problem it solves:
- Eliminates manual management via messaging or calls that generates double bookings and lost traceability.
- Allows clients to book autonomously in real-time.
- Delivers complete operational visibility of each appointment status.
- Guarantees at database level that a time slot cannot be booked twice.

Technical approach:
- Concurrency control with atomic transactions and select_for_update().
- Data integrity with UniqueConstraint on bookings and availability slots.
- Cross-cutting security with rate limiting middleware and security headers.
- Production operation with multi-environment configuration (SQLite in development and PostgreSQL in production).
highlights: Concurrency control with SELECT FOR UPDATE to eliminate race conditions
UniqueConstraint on Booking and AvailabilitySlot to prevent double bookings in DB
IP-based rate limiting on login, registration, and booking creation with HTTP 429 response
Security middleware with HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options
Structured logging of critical events (registration, booking, rate limit exceeded, conflicts)
Production-ready configuration with DEBUG=False, environment-based SECRET_KEY, and dynamic ALLOWED_HOSTS
Modular architecture by domain apps without circular dependencies
Containerized deployment with Docker + Render for stable production environment

architecture: core: global configuration, security middleware, and root routing
bookings: Booking creation and administrative dashboard
pages: editable public content (about us and gallery)
Django ORM: exclusive data access with relational model
dataFlow: Client registers and ClientProfile is created associated with user.
Selects service, date, and queries active unreserved slots.
On confirmation, system opens transaction.atomic() and locks slot with select_for_update().
If slot remains free, Booking is persisted in pending state; otherwise, conflict is reported.
Administration manages states (pending, confirmed, completed, cancelled).
Middlewares apply rate limit, security headers, and critical event auditing.

sales-inventory-admin-system.title: Sales and Inventory Management System
sales-inventory-admin-system.shortDescription: Enterprise web system for managing inventory, sales, supplier entries, customers, and reports with auditing and role-based control.
sales-inventory-admin-system.fullDescription:
Business administration web system developed as university thesis project.

Problem it solves:
- Replaces manual spreadsheet management with centralized multi-user platform.
- Prevents stock inconsistencies and lack of traceability in commercial operations.
- Provides role-based access control, data integrity, and cross-cutting auditing.

Technical approach:
- Comprehensive operational cycle management: products, orders, entries, customers, and reports.
- Critical operations in atomic transactions to preserve inventory consistency.
- Reports endpoint with dynamic filters and complex joins for commercial analysis.
- HTTP security hardening and automatic activity logging per user and model.
highlights: Reusable RegistraActividad trait for automatic auditing (created, updated, deleted)
RBAC with three levels (Admin, Manager, Sales) and granular action permissions
DB::transaction() in order creation for stock and total atomicity
M:N Order-Product relationship with pedido_producto pivot (quantity, gift)
JSON endpoint /reportes/ventas-productos-data with joins, conditional filters, and grouping
Listings with pagination and combined filters without full page reload
HTTP hardening with CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options
Scalable MVC architecture with clear separation of concerns

architecture: Eloquent Models: User, Product, Category, Brand, Customer, Order, Entry, ActivityHistory
Controllers per module: products, orders, entries, reports, users, and roles
Blade views per domain with shared layout for consistent UI
RBAC authorization with auth middleware + Spatie Laravel Permission
Cross-cutting audit layer with RegistraActividad trait on Eloquent events
HTTP security layer with secure-headers aligned to OWASP
dataFlow: User logs in and system enables actions based on role (Admin, Manager, Sales).
From dashboard, sales indicators, customers, orders, and notifications are consumed.
When creating order, products and quantities are added; a transaction updates stock and totals with VAT.
When registering entry, supplier-product is associated and inventory is updated with traceability.
Reports module executes queries with joins and filters by date, brand, and category.
Views consume JSON endpoint for dynamic product sales analytics.
Each CRUD operation triggers automatic auditing with user, IP, and previous/new changes.
